title: lem:ckm: 'G^kappa_mu empty or singleton' stated without proof

comments.author: human
comments.body:
`lem:ckm` asserts that 'since $\lambda$ has distinct parts, $\mathcal G^\kappa_\mu$ is empty or a single element.' This uniqueness is essential: it is what makes $c^\kappa_\nu$ a single unsigned-object weight (not a sum), and `prop:step2` explicitly invokes 'the unique element of $\mathcal G^\rho_\nu$.'

No argument is given. A combinatorial proof is needed that distinct parts of $\lambda$ force at most one compatible unsigned two-line queue with prescribed top row $\kappa$ and bottom row $\mu$. Also, the related `lem:signforget` should make explicit that the admissible sign choices and the $b=a$ tie case are exhausted correctly (the case $b=a$ cannot occur for distinct parts among positive labels, but vacancies/label-0 interactions need care).
